rationale: Nginx uses a multi-process architecture with a master process and multiple worker processes, all sharing the 'nginx' comm name. In a CPU-oversubscribed environment (e.g., co-located with CPU-intensive batch jobs like stress-ng), nginx worker processes compete for CPU time with CPU hog processes. Deprioritizing known CPU hog processes (stress-ng) ensures nginx workers get scheduled promptly, reducing P99 request latency without starving background compute (which is throughput-oriented, not latency-sensitive).

threads.purpose: Nginx master and worker processes. Workers handle HTTP request processing via an event-driven model. Each worker serves thousands of concurrent connections. Latency-critical — scheduling delays directly increase HTTP response time.
CPU-intensive batch workload co-located with nginx. Represents any CPU hog process that competes with nginx for CPU time. Not part of nginx itself but must be deprioritized to protect nginx latency.
